options.text: This solution will deploy a fully functional Azure Virtual Desktop Hostpool with associated resources. The resources deployed are dependent upon your choices within the form. All resources and resource groups are named in accordance with Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) naming standards.
This solution has several prerequisites documented in the Repo.

elements.name: avdObjectId
elements.label: AVD Object Id
elements.toolTip: Search your Entra ID tenant for the Azure Virtual Desktop or Windows Virtual Desktop enterprise Application with Applicaton ID (9cdead84-a844-4324-93f2-b2e6bb768d07) and enter the Object Id here.

constraints.validationMessage: A proper GUID must be provided.

options.text: Please provide the naming components below. The Business Unit Identifier is optional and specifically used to uniquely identify resources and resource groups between business units in a shared AVD subscription. If the business unit identifier is not specified (left blank), then it is assumed the subscription delineates the business unit and therefore centralized monitoring and management is assumed and resources and resource groups are named accordingly.

elements.name: envShortName
elements.label: Environment
elements.toolTip: Select the target environment for the solution: Development (d), Test (t), Production (p). The abbreviation will be used as part of the naming convention for the resoure groups and resources.

elements.name: businessUnitIdentifier
elements.label: Business Unit Identifier
elements.toolTip: Input an alphanumeric identifier for the business unit utilizing AVD in your tenant.

elements.name: hostPoolIdentifier
elements.label: HostPool Identifier
elements.toolTip: Input a alphanumeric identifier for the hostpool created by this solution.

elements.name: centralizedAVDMonitoring
elements.label: Centralized AVD Monitoring
elements.toolTip: When the 'businessUnitIdentifier' parameter is not empty, this parameter determines if the AVD Monitoring Resource Group and associated resources are created in a centralized resource group (does not include 'Business Unit Identifier' in the name) and monitoring resources are named accordingly or if a Business unit specific AVD monitoring resource group is created and monitoring resources are named accordingly. If the 'Business Unit Identifier' value is left empty, then this value has no effect.

options.text: The Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) specifies some best practices when naming resources. Some customers have chosen to reverse this order of naming elements by placing the resource type at the end of the naming convention. This solution allows you to select the name of most resources, but there are several that are automatically named including Private Endpoints. Select 'Yes' below to ensure the automatically named resources are named with this reverse convention or leave it set to 'No' to follow the CAF.

elements.name: nameConvResTypeAtEnd
elements.label: Move the Resource Type to the end of resource names

options.text: There is an existing AVD workspace that matches the naming convention and is in the control plane region you selected. This solution allows you to update the application group references of an existing AVD Workspace or create a new Workspace. Select the appropriate option below.

When you choose create to create a new workspace this solution will overwrite any existing application group references <u>if the workspace already exists</u>. This could cause an outage if existing hostpools and application groups are already registered with this workspace.
Select an existing AVD Workspace below to which the Desktop Application Group will be registered.

elements.name: existingWorkspace
elements.label: Existing AVD Workspace
elements.toolTip: Select the existing AVD Workspace to update with the Desktop Application Group deployed via this solution.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('controlPlane').workspacesApi.value, (ws) => equals(ws.location, steps('controlPlane').location.name)), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Friendly Name: ', item.properties.friendlyName, ', Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: workspaceFriendlyName
elements.label: Workspace Friendly Name
elements.toolTip: Input the friendly name for the AVD workspace that will be displayed in the end user's client.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must be between 1 and 64 characters in length.

elements.name: desktopFriendlyName
elements.label: Session Desktop Friendly Name
elements.toolTip: Input the friendly name for the default SessionDesktop that will be displayed in the end user's client.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must be between 1 and 20 characters in length.

elements.name: validation
elements.label: Validation Environment
elements.toolTip: Choose whether to deploy the host pool as a validation environment. This allows you test preview features for AVD before they are released to production.

elements.name: loadBalancerAlgorithm
elements.label: Load Balancing Algorithm
elements.toolTip: Breadth-first load balancing distributes new user sessions across all available session hosts in the host pool. Depth-first load balancing distributes new user sessions to an available session host with the highest number of connections but has not reached its maximum session limit threshold.

allowedValues.label: Breadth-First
allowedValues.description: Each new session is placed on the next VM. (Performance Optimized)

allowedValues.label: Depth-First
allowedValues.description: Each new session is placed on the same VM until max sessions limit. (Cost Optimized)

elements.name: rdpProperties
elements.label: Custom RDP properties
elements.defaultValue: audiocapturemode:i:1;camerastoredirect:s:*;use multimon:i:0;drivestoredirect:s:;encode redirected video capture:i:1;redirected video capture encoding quality:i:1;audiomode:i:0;devicestoredirect:s:;redirectclipboard:i:0;redirectcomports:i:0;redirectlocation:i:1;redirectprinters:i:0;redirectsmartcards:i:1;redirectwebauthn:i:1;usbdevicestoredirect:s:;keyboardhook:i:2;
elements.toolTip: Specify the configuration for the RDP properties on the AVD host pool.

elements.name: maxSessions
elements.label: Max Session Limit
elements.toolTip: The maximum number of users that have concurrent sessions on a session host. When setting a host pool to have depth first load balancing or planning to use Autoscaling, you must set an appropriate max session limit according to the configuration of your deployment and capacity of your VMs.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must be one or more digits.

elements.name: assignmentType
elements.label: Assignment Type
elements.toolTip: Automatic assignment - The service will select an available host and assign it to an user./nDirect assignment - Admin selects a specific host to assign to an user.

allowedValues.label: Automatic (Recommended)
allowedValues.description: Users are assigned an available VM the first time they connect.

allowedValues.label: Direct
allowedValues.description: An administrator assigns a VM for each individual user.

elements.name: groupsDropDown
elements.label: Groups
elements.toolTip: Select the desired group(s) to give access to this AVD hostpool and if applicable, the FSLogix file share.

elements.name: groupsGrid
elements.ariaLabel: Enter the security groups for access to AVD and if applicable, FSLogix. If deploying FSLogix, a storage account will be deployed for each group to support sharding.
elements.label: Security Groups

elements.name: deployScalingPlan
elements.label: Deploy Scaling Plan
elements.toolTip: Select Yes to configure and deploy a scaling plan specific to the host pool.

elements.name: exclusionTag
elements.label: Tag to exclude hosts from Scaling
elements.toolTip: Use only allowed characters

constraints.validationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters are allowed, and the value must be 1-30 characters long.

options.text: Use the grids and selectors below to configure the Weekday Scaling Plan schedule.

validations.message: Must be a valid 24-hour time format without leading zeros.
Must be a valid 24-hour time format without leading zeros.
Must be a valid 24-hour time format without leading zeros.

elements.name: minsToLogoff
elements.label: Number of minutes before users are forcefully logged off
elements.toolTip: Use only integers

elements.name: weekdayOffPeakSchedule
elements.ariaLabel: Enter Weekday Off Peak Schedule Information
elements.label: Weekday Off Peak Schedule

validations.message: Must be a valid 24-hour time format without leading zeros.

elements.name: drainMode
elements.label: Drain Mode
elements.toolTip: Enables drain mode on the AVD session hosts so the virtual machines cannot be accessed until they have been validated.

options.text: Naming

elements.name: virtualMachineNamePrefix
elements.label: Virtual Machine Name Prefix
elements.toolTip: Provide a Virtual Machine name prefix consisting of alphanumeric and dashes up to 12 characters long. A three character numeric suffix will be appended to the name padded with zeros (0).

constraints.validationMessage: The prefix must begin with an alphanumeric character and then contain only alphanumeric characters and dashes. The value must also be less than 12 characters long.

elements.name: index
elements.label: Starting number (index)
elements.toolTip: Select the start number of the virtual machine suffix.

elements.name: hostsVirtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network to which the session hosts will be attached.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('hosts').network.hostsVirtualNetworksApi.value, (item) => equals(item.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Location: ', item.location, ' Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: hostsSubnet
elements.label: Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select an existing subnet for the AVD session hosts.

elements.name: securityType
elements.label: Security Type
elements.toolTip: Select the appropriate Security Type configuration for the Virtual Machine. Note that 'Trusted Launch' and 'Confidential Virtual Machines' are not available in all regions and may require specific VM Sizes.

options.text: Confidential VMs are not supported in all regions and require certain VM Skus.

elements.name: secureBootEnabled
elements.label: Enable Secure Boot
elements.toolTip: Secure boot helps protect your VMs against boot kits, rootkits, and kernel-level malware.

elements.name: vTpmEnabled
elements.label: Enable vTPM
elements.toolTip: Virtual Trusted Platform Module (vTPM) is TPM2.0 compliant and validates your VM boot integrity apart from securely storing keys and secrets.

elements.name: integrityMonitoring
elements.label: Integrity Monitoring
elements.toolTip: Integrity monitoring enables cryptographic attestation and verification of VM boot integrity along with monitoring alerts if the VM didn't boot because attestation failed with the defined baseline.

options.text: There are several types of encryption available for your managed disks including Azure Disk Storage Server-Side Encryption (SSE), Encryption at Host (EAH), and Confidential disk encryption (when the Confidential VM security type is selected).
• Azure Disk Storage Server-Side Encryption is always enabled and automatically encrypts your data stored on Azure managed disks (OS and data disks) when persisting on the storage clusters.
• Encryption at host enhances Azure Disk Storage Server-Side Encryption to ensure that all temp disks and disk caches are encrypted at rest and flow encrypted to the Storage clusters.

• Confidential disk encryption binds disk encryption keys to the virtual machine's TPM and makes the protected disk content accessible only to the VM. The TPM and VM guest state is always encrypted in attested code using keys released by a secure protocol that bypasses the hypervisor and host operating system. Currently only available for the OS disk. Encryption at host may be used for other disks on a Confidential VM in addition to Confidential Disk Encryption.

elements.name: encryptionAtHost
elements.label: Encryption At Host
elements.toolTip: Check to enable Encryption At Host

elements.name: confidentialVMOSDiskEncryption
elements.label: Confidential OS Disk Encryption
elements.toolTip: Check to enable Confidential VM disk encryption

options.text: You can rely on platform-managed keys for the encryption of your managed disk, or you can manage encryption using your own keys. If you choose to manage encryption with your own keys, you can specify a customer-managed key to use for encrypting and decrypting all data in managed disks.

In some environments it is required to utilize Customer Managed Keys and protect those keys in a premium key vault with the keys stored in a FIPS 140 validated Hardware Security Module. This is most common in IL5 type environments. Selecting a Customer Managed Key Hardware Security Module (HSM) option will ensure that all VM disks are protected with this configuration. Click this box for more information.

elements.name: keyManagement
elements.label: Key Management
elements.toolTip: Platform-managed keys are key encryption keys that are generated, stored, and managed entirely by Azure. Choose Platform Managed for the best balance of security and ease of use. Customer-managed keys are key encryption keys that are generated, stored, and managed by you, the customer, in your Azure Key Vault. Choose Customer Managed if you need to meet specific compliance requirements. Platform-Managed and Customer-Managed Keys provide double encryption with an infrastructure encryption layer using platform-managed keys and a disk encryption layer using customer-managed keys.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), parse('[{"label":"Platform-Managed Keys","value":"PlatformManaged"},{"label":"Customer-Managed Keys protected by HSM","value":"CustomerManaged"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Platform-Managed Keys","value":"PlatformManaged"},{"label":"Customer-Managed Keys","value":"CustomerManaged"},{"label":"Customer-Managed Keys protected by HSM","value":"CustomerManagedHSM"},{"label":"Platform-Managed and Customer-Managed Keys","value":"PlatformManagedAndCustomerManaged"},{"label":"Platform-Managed and Customer-Managed Keys protected by HSM","value":"PlatformManagedAndCustomerManagedHSM"}]'))]

options.text: You must have completed the prerequistes to support Confidential VM Disk Encryption.

elements.name: confDiskEncryptionPrincipal
elements.label: Confidential VM Orchestrator (Object Id)
elements.toolTip: Enter the Object Id of the Confidential VM Orchestrator Service Principal with AppId 'bf7b6499-ff71-4aa2-97a4-f372087be7f0'

constraints.validationMessage: A proper GUID must be provided.

options.text: Azure Dedicated Host is a service that provides physical servers able to host one or more virtual machines assigned to one Azure subscription. Dedicated hosts are the same physical servers used in our data centers, provided instead as a directly accessible hardware resource.

In some environments it is required to have a dedicated Azure Host to isolate Compute. This is most common in IL5 type environments. Selecting this option will ensure the VMs are deployed to pre-existing Dedicated Host(s). Click this box for more information.

elements.name: deployToDedicatedHosts
elements.label: Deploy to Dedicated Hosts
elements.toolTip: Select this check box to deploy the Virtual Machines to existing dedicated host(s).

options.text: Select the dedicated host group and optionally the specific dedicated host below. Leave the dedicated host blank if you want to use automatic host selection.

elements.name: dedicatedHostGroup
elements.label: Dedicated Host Group
elements.toolTip: Select the dedicated host group to which the Virtual Machines will be deployed using automatic host selection.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('hosts').dedicatedHosts.hostGroupsApi.value, (item) => equals(item.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), ', Availability Zone: ', item.zones, '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: dedicatedHost
elements.label: Dedicated Host (Manual placement only)
elements.toolTip: This selection is optional. Do not select a host if you wish to use automatic selection.

elements.name: count
elements.label: Number of Virtual Machines
elements.toolTip: Select the number of virtual machines to deploy in your AVD host pool.

elements.name: diskSku
elements.label: OS Disk SKU
elements.toolTip: Select the disk SKU for the operating system disk.

elements.name: sizeGeneric
elements.label: Size
elements.toolTip: Select the size of the virtual machines. Multi-session hosts should have 4 - 24 vCPUs. Single session host should have 2 or more vCPUs.

elements.name: sizeConfidentialVM
elements.label: Virtual Machine Size
elements.toolTip: Select the size of the virtual machines. Multi-session hosts should have 4 - 24 vCPUs. Single session host should have 2 or more vCPUs.

elements.name: hibernationEnabled
elements.label: Enable Hibernation
elements.toolTip: Hibernation allows you to pause VMs that aren't being used and save on compute costs where the VMs don't need to run 24/7.
elements.visible: [and(equals(steps('controlPlane').hostPool.type, 'Personal'), bool(first(map(filter(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').specs.resourceSkusApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (item) => item.capabilities)), (item) => equals(item.name, 'HibernationSupported')), (item) => item.value))))]

elements.name: availability
elements.defaultValue: [if(empty(first(map(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').specs.resourceSkusApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (item) => item.locationInfo)), (item) => item.zones))), 'No infrastructure redundancy required', 'Availability Zones')]
elements.label: Availability Options
elements.toolTip: Select the redundancy / resiliency for the virtual machines.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(empty(first(map(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').specs.resourceSkusApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (item) => item.locationInfo)), (item) => item.zones))), parse('[{"label":"No infrastructure redundancy required","value":"None"},{"label":"Availability Sets","value":"AvailabilitySets"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Availability Zones","value":"AvailabilityZones"},{"label":"Availability Sets","value":"AvailabilitySets"},{"label":"No infrastructure redundancy required","value":"None"}]'))]

options.text: The number of storage accounts are automatically determined based on your choices from previous screens. The storage account names are automatically determined by combining a suffix with a 0-padded two digit suffix starting at the 'Storage Index' value.

For the 'Entra Id' identity provider selected on the 'Session Host' screen, the storage account count value is limited to '1'. You can deploy a maximum of 1 storage account when the identity provider is 'EntraId' because sharding is not possible.

link.label: Microsoft Learn | FSLogix - How To Configure Object Specific Settings

options.text: For the 'Active Directory Domain Services' or 'Entra Id Domain Services' identity providers selected on the 'Session Host' screen, the number of storage accounts equals the number of security principals or groups you entered under the assignments section of the 'Control Plane' blade. A storage account is created for each security principal and permissions are assigned separately to each to enable storage sharding.

link.label: Microsoft Learn | FSLogix - How To Configure Object Specific Settings

options.text: You can deploy a maximum of 4 storage accounts when using Cloud Cache. The number of groups/principals assigned in the 'Assignment' pane is over 4. Either reduce the number of principals assigned, or do not use Cloud Cache as a container type.

elements.label: Storage Count

elements.name: storageIndex
elements.label: Storage Index

elements.name: storageKerberosEncryption

options.text: Encryption Key Management

link.label: Microsoft Learn | Azure Storage - Customer Managed Keys

options.text: Azure Storage uses service-side encryption (SSE) to automatically encrypt your data when it is persisted to the cloud. Data in storage accounts is encrypted with Microsoft-managed keys by default. You can continue to rely on Microsoft-managed keys for the encryption of your data, or you can manage encryption with your own keys. You can specify a customer-managed key to use for encrypting and decrypting data in Blob Storage and in Azure Files. Customer-managed keys must be stored in Azure Key Vault or Azure Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Model (HSM).

In some environments it is required to utilize Customer Managed Keys and protect those keys in a premium key vault with the keys stored in a FIPS 140 validated Hardware Security Module. This is most common in IL5 type environments. Selecting a Customer Managed Key Hardware Security Module (HSM) option will ensure that all storage accounts protected with this configuration. Click this box for more information.

elements.name: keyManagement
elements.label: Storage Encryption Key Management
elements.defaultValue: [if(contains(steps('hosts').diskEncryption.keyManagement, 'Customer'), if(contains(steps('hosts').diskEncryption.keyManagement, 'HSM'), 'Customer-Managed Keys protected by HSM', 'Customer-Managed Keys'), 'Microsoft-Managed Keys')]
elements.toolTip: Select the type of Key Management to use for the FSLogix storage account(s) encryption keys.

elements.name: recoveryServices
elements.label: Enable Recovery Services
elements.toolTip: Choose to deploy backups for your solution. For a personal host pool, this will enable backups on the virtual machines. For a pooled host pool, this will enable backups on the FSLogix file share when using Azure Files.

elements.name: enableMonitoring
elements.label: Enable Monitoring for AVD and VM Insights
elements.toolTip: Deploy the required resources to enable AVD and VM Insights.

elements.name: enableSecurity

elements.toolTip: Deploy the Log Analytics (Microsoft Monitoring) Agent or a Data Collection Rule Association to use the Azure Monitoring Agent for collecting security relevant data.

elements.name: dataCollectionRule
elements.label: Existing Data Collection Rule for Security
elements.toolTip: Select the Data Collection Rule used for collecting security data for Sentinel or Defender for Cloud. This is required to create the Data Collection Rule Association

elements.name: size
elements.label: Virtual Machine Size
elements.toolTip: Select the size of the management virtual machine. This host should be the smallest sku available and in the B series for the least cost.

options.text: Zero Trust is a new security model that assumes breach and verifies each request as though it originated from an uncontrolled network.

Private Endpoints are one key technology we can use to implement Zero Trust Architecture Guidance. Private endpoints should be utilized for all Platform as a Service (PaaS) resources (i.e., Automation Accounts, Blob Storage, File Storage, and Key Vaults utilized by this solution. Private Endpoints have their own set of prereqs including Private DNS Zones.

link.label: Microsoft Learn | Private Link Deployment in a Hub and Spoke Network

options.text:
When deploying private endpoints, you must select the subnet to which they will be attached and the Private DNS Zones to enable DNS resolution. Since this deployment expects that an Azure Landing Zone has already been deployed, you may be utilizing multiple subscriptions for different components of your architecture. To support this, the following drop downs allow you to select the subscription(s) containing the subnets and the Private DNS zones required to support Zero Trust. Once you select the subscription(s), you'll be able to select the virtual network, subnet, and appropropriate Private DNS Zones from these subscriptions. The following Private DNS Zones may be required:
  • Azure Files for FSLogix profile storage (privatelink.file.suffix)
  • Azure Key Vault (privatelink.vaultcore.suffix)
  • Automation Accounts for the Storage Auto Quota Increase capability (privatelink.azure-automation.suffix)
  • Azure Blob Storage for Disk Access and artifacts storage (privatelink.blob.suffix)
See the documentation in the source repo to determine the correct fully-qualified domain name for each zone based on the cloud.

options.text: [if(and(equals(steps('controlPlane').hostPool.type, 'Personal'), steps('management').backup.recoveryServices, steps('zeroTrust').privateEndpoints.deployPrivateEndpoints), 'Check the following box to define and and assign a policy that disables public network access and automatically assigns a disk access resource to the virtual machine OS disks for private access.', 'Select the following check box to define and assign a policy that disables all network access to your managed disks.')]

link.label: Microsoft Learn | Restrict Managed Disks from Being Imported or Exported
Microsoft Learn | AVD Private Link Overview

options.text: You can use Azure Private Link with Azure Virtual Desktop to privately connect to your remote resources. By creating a private endpoint, traffic between your virtual network and the service remains on the Microsoft network, so you no longer need to expose your service to the public internet. You also use a VPN or ExpressRoute for your users with the Remote Desktop client to connect to the virtual network. Keeping traffic within the Microsoft network improves security and keeps your data safe.

link.label: Microsoft Learn | AVD Private Link Overview

options.text:
When enabling Private Link with Azure Virtual Desktop, you have the following supported scenarios to connect to Azure Virtual Desktop. Choose the configuration below to meet your requirements.
  1. All parts of the connection - initial feed discovery, feed download, and remote session connections - use private routes.

  2. Feed download and remote session connections use private routes, but initial feed discovery uses public routes.

  3. Only remote session connections use private routes, but initial feed discovery and feed download use public routes.

[concat('An existing global feed workspace has been detected: ', first(map(filter(steps('controlPlane').workspacesApi.value, (ws) => contains(ws.name, 'global-feed')), (ws) => ws.name)), '. Therefore, you can only choose either option 2 or 3, because you can only have one global-feed workspace with one private endpoint.')]

elements.name: avdPrivateLinkConfig
elements.label: AVD Private Link Configuration
elements.defaultValue: [if(empty(filter(steps('controlPlane').workspacesApi.value, (ws) => contains(ws.name, 'global-feed'))), 'All parts of the connection use private routes', if(equals(steps('controlPlane').workspace.createOption, 'update'), 'Only remote session connections use private routes', 'Feed download and remote session connections use private routes'))]
elements.toolTip: Chooose the AVD Private Link Configuration.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(empty(filter(steps('controlPlane').workspacesApi.value, (ws) => contains(ws.name, 'global-feed'))), parse('[{"label":"All parts of the connection use private routes","description":"Global feed, Workspace feed, and hostpool private endpoints are required","value":"All"},{"label":"Feed download and remote session connections use private routes","description":"Workspace feed and Hostpool private endpoints are required","value":"FeedAndHostpool"},{"label":"Only remote session connections use private routes","description":"Hostpool private endpoints are required","value":"Hostpool"}]'), parse('[{"label":"Feed download and remote session connections use private routes","description":"Workspace feed and Hostpool private endpoints are required","value":"FeedAndHostpool"},{"label":"Only remote session connections use private routes","description":"Hostpool private endpoints are required","value":"Hostpool"}]'))]

options.text: Because you have chosen to update an existing AVD workspace on the Control Plane blade, use caution when updating the AVD Private Link configuration for the Workspace Feed. You can only have one private endpoint per workspace; therefore, if your workspace is already configured with a private endpoint to support private routes for the workspace feed, it is best to select "Only remote session connections use private routes" as the AVD Private Link Configuration. The "Feed download and remote session connections use private routes" option is primarily provided to allow an existing workspace to be updated with this option.
Remote Session Connections
Select the subscription and private DNS zone for remote session connections and workspace feed download (if applicable).

elements.name: hostpoolSubnet
elements.label: Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select an existing subnet for the private Endpoints.

elements.name: hostpoolPublicNetworkAccess
elements.label: Remote Session Connection public access
elements.toolTip: Select the appropriate option for configuring public network access to the remote session.

allowedValues.label: Disabled
allowedValues.description: Remote sessions are denied when either the client or session host is using a public route.

allowedValues.label: Enabled
allowedValues.description: Remote sessions are allowed when either the client or session host are using a private or public route.

allowedValues.label: Enabled For Clients Only
allowedValues.description: Remote sessions are allowed as long as the session host is using a private route, regardless of the route the client is using.

options.text: Workspace Feed

Select the subscription, virtual network, and subnet for the workspace feed private endpoint. The virtual network is limited to the control plane location as specified on the 'Control Plane' pane. The same private dns zone as the remote session connection is used.

parameters.enableAcceleratedNetworking: [bool(first(map(filter(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').specs.resourceSkusApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (item) => item.capabilities)), (item) => equals(item.name, 'AcceleratedNetworkingEnabled')), (item) => item.value)))]
parameters.hibernationEnabled: [if(bool(first(map(filter(first(map(filter(steps('hosts').specs.resourceSkusApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), steps('hosts').specs.sizeConfidentialVM, steps('hosts').specs.sizeGeneric))), (item) => item.capabilities)), (item) => equals(item.name, 'HibernationSupported')), (item) => item.value))), steps('hosts').specs.hibernationEnabled, false)]
